item.title: Deutsche Bank Raises 10-Year Treasury Yield Forecast on Fed View
item.description: Interest-rate strategists at Deutsche Bank AG boosted their forecast for the 10-year Treasury yield into the end of the year on the expectation that Federal Reserve officials now led by Chairman Kevin Warsh are finished cutting interest rates.

item.title: Why One of This Year’s Best Games, ‘Mina the Hollower,’ Is Only $20
item.description: The developers at Yacht Club say that charging $20 for their slick new platformer was a no-brainer

item.title: Space Stocks’ Hype Gets Reality Check With Blue Origin Blowup
item.description: A weeks-long rally in space-related stocks came to an abrupt halt on Friday, with the explosion of a Blue Origin rocket serving as a glaring reminder of the risks that come with the potential riches investors hope to reap from the blossoming industry.

item.title: Lula Rejects US Terrorism Designation for Brazil Drug Gangs
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva blasted a US decision to label two major Brazilian criminal groups as terrorist organizations, calling it a threat to the nation’s sovereignty that won’t help its fight against drug trafficking.

item.title: Rio Tinto’s Aluminum Exports to US Rebound to Pretariff Levels
item.description: One of the world’s largest aluminum producers, Rio Tinto Group, says its shipments of the lightweight metal to the US have bounced back to levels from before President Donald Trump’s tariff offensive.

item.title: Treasuries Set for Best Week Since War Began as Oil Retreats
item.description: The US government bond market headed for its best week since the start of the US war on Iran as oil prices declined in anticipation of an agreement to end the conflict.

item.title: Canada Aims to Boost China Trade as Wang Makes First Visit in Decade
item.description: Foreign Minister Anita Anand said Canada aims to grow exports to China by 50% by 2030, as her Chinese counterpart visits Ottawa in a sign of a shifting global order shaped by US tariffs.

item.title: Iceland’s Own Currency Costs Outweigh Benefits, Ministry Says
item.description: The drawbacks to Iceland from having its own currency likely exceed the benefits, the country’s Finance Ministry said, citing the conclusions of a government-commissioned report.

item.title: US Paves Way for Onshore Crypto-Linked Perpetual Futures
item.description: The main US derivatives regulator said perpetual futures contracts for cryptocurrencies could trade on registered onshore platforms if they meet certain conditions, bringing to America a popular trading instrument that has until now existed almost entirely overseas.

item.title: BlackRock Scales Back Equities After ‘Generational’ Earnings
item.description: BlackRock Inc. is trimming its bet on stocks across its $220 billion model-portfolio business as US equities surge to record highs following a strong earnings season.

item.title: EU’s Economic Powers Strike Deal to Push Capital Markets Merger
item.description: The European Union’s six largest economies have agreed on a plan to implement a bloc-wide capital markets union, reaching a collective stance they say will help spur the continent to action after years of delay.

item.title: Petra Begins Finsch Mine Rescue Plan Amid Diamond Market Slump
item.description: Petra Diamonds Ltd. is putting one of its two mines into a business restructuring process and is considering job cuts across the rest of the company, as falling diamond prices and a strong rand erode earnings and liquidity.

item.title: Ford Stock Set for Best Month Since Financial Crisis on AI Rally
item.description: Ford Motor Co. shares are set to record their best monthly gain in 17 years as the automaker’s newfound status as a possible beneficiary of the artificial intelligence boom sparked an investor frenzy.

item.title: HSBC’s Kettner Says Blowout Earnings Dim ‘Danger Zone’ Fears
item.description: Robust first-quarter corporate results, driven by technology megacaps, have provided fundamental support to the stock-market rally, dimming worries about elevated Treasury yields, according to HSBC Bank Plc’s Max Kettner.

item.title: Quarter of Big Oil Tankers Trapped by Iran War Have Escaped
item.description: Roughly one-quarter of the non-Iranian large oil tankers trapped inside the Persian Gulf at the outbreak of the Iran war have managed to slip out in a slow, stealthy trickle.

item.title: Vietnam’s To Lam Says Military Power Alone Can’t Ensure Security
item.description: Vietnamese leader To Lam told a major Asian defense forum Friday that military power alone can’t ensure security, and called for the creation of mechanisms to actively prevent conflicts from spiraling.

item.title: Hungary President Signals He Won’t Resign in Challenge to Magyar
item.description: Hungarian President Tamas Sulyok signaled he has no intention of stepping down, setting the stage for a confrontation between the country’s new government and institutions still controlled by appointees of former leader Viktor Orban.

item.title: Stocks Rally Continues on Peace Deal Hopes, AI Spending Optimism
item.description: US stocks edged higher in early trading on Friday, boosted by hopes of a peace deal between the US and Iran and expectations that spending on artificial intelligence will drive further gains in corporate earnings.

item.title: ECB Acting Too Late Can Be Costlier Than Acting Earlier, Radev Says
item.description: The European Central Bank should avoid waiting too long to address the fallout from the Iran war, according to Governing Council member Dimitar Radev.

item.title: Shutterfly Kicks Off $1.9 Billion Junk Deal to Tackle Debt Pile
item.description: Shutterfly is looking to refinance its looming debt pile with $1.875 billion of new junk bonds and loans.

item.title: FitzWalter Bought Bank Debt of Ailing 3i-Backed Fiber Operator
item.description: Special situations investor FitzWalter Capital has bought around half of the bank debt owed by struggling German fiber operator DNS:Net, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fed’s Bowman Says Too Soon to Judge Inflation Impact of Iran War
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said it’s too soon to judge the inflationary impact from the Iran war and that policymakers need to look through temporary price shocks.

item.title: Zambia Bonds Jump After 2053 Debt Buyback Tender Starts
item.description: Zambia launched a cash tender offer to buy back $1.36 billion bond due in 2053, becoming the latest African nation to repurchase outstanding debt as governments seek to reduce their servicing costs.

item.title: US Merchandise-Trade Deficit Narrows on Rise in Exports
item.description: The US merchandise-trade deficit narrowed in April, reflecting an increase in the value of exports of capital and consumer goods.

item.title: Canada Dips Into Technical Recession for First Time Since 2020
item.description: The Canadian economy edged into a technical recession as weak business and government spending drove a slight contraction in the first quarter.

item.title: ‘Two Sigma’ S&P Rally Signals Easy Money Has Already Been Made
item.description: The S&P 500 Index has returned to all-time highs, yet there’s no mistaking that Wall Street’s breathtaking two-month rally has slowed dramatically.

item.title: India’s Maritime Financier Readies Nation’s First Blue Bond
item.description: Sagarmala Finance Corp., a financier created to fund India’s maritime projects, is preparing to issue the country’s maiden blue bond to raise as much as 10 billion rupees ($105 million), according to a top official.

item.title: Sugar Climbs Most in a Month on Poor India Monsoon Rain Outlook
item.description: Sugar futures in New York posted the biggest gain in a month on concerns that an emerging El Niño weather pattern could curb rainfall and damage the cane crop in India, the world’s second-largest producer.

item.title: Brazil’s Economy Rebounds as Lula Cranks Up Aid Before Election
item.description: Brazil’s economy picked up more than expected at the start of the year, powered by government stimulus and robust mining that fueled growth in the face of double-digit borrowing costs.

item.title: Unknown MAGA Warrior Upends Race for a Seat on Texas Oil Regulator
item.description: Hard-right candidate with little connection to the oil industry is primed to win a place on the Railroad Commission of Texas.
